word_list.word: a chain is only as strong as its weakest link
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ tʃˈeɪn ɪz ˈoʊnli æz stɹˈɔŋ æz ɪts wˈiːkəst lˈɪŋk

senses.definition: used to imply that the effectiveness and success of a group or system is dependent on the performance of its weakest member
used to imply that both parties involved in a situation or interaction are equally responsible for the outcome and must cooperate and contribute equally to achieve success
used to suggest that when people work together, a task becomes easier and quicker to complete, as collective effort can achieve more in less time than individual effort
used to emphasize that even small contributions or efforts can make a difference and be of value in achieving a larger goal

examples.x: When it comes to reducing waste, every little helps, from using reusable bags to recycling paper and plastic.

word_list.word: a rising tide lifts all boats
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɹˈaɪzɪŋ tˈaɪd lˈɪfts ˈɔːl bˈoʊts

senses.definition: used to imply that positive changes or progress in a system or community will benefit all those within it, regardless of their position

word_list.word: even a mouse may help a lion
word_list.phonetic_text: i.vɪn ə maʊs meɪ hɛlp ə laɪən
word_list.phonetic_am_text: i.vin ē maoos mei help ē laiēn
word_list.pronunciation: ˈiːvən ɐ mˈaʊs mˈeɪ hˈɛlp ɐ lˈaɪən

senses.definition: used to imply that even those who are considered small or weak in a group can provide useful assistance or support to those who are dominant or powerful
used to imply that individuals cannot be completely self-sufficient and need the company and support of others in order to thrive and succeed in life

word_list.word: one beats the bush, and another catches the birds
word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌn bˈiːts ðə bˈʊʃ ænd ɐnˈʌðɚ kˈætʃᵻz ðə bˈɜːdz

senses.definition: used to emphasize cooperation and coordination for a common goal, where individuals contribute their unique skills to achieve desired outcomes

word_list.word: one hand washes the other (and together they wash the face|)
word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌn hˈænd wˈɑːʃᵻz ðɪ ˈʌðɚ ænd təɡˌɛðɚ ðeɪ wˈɑːʃ ðə fˈeɪs

senses.definition: used to imply that by cooperating and exchanging favors, all parties involved can benefit and achieve more than they could individually

word_list.word: two heads are better than one
word_list.pronunciation: tˈuː hˈɛdz ɑːɹ bˈɛɾɚ ðɐn wˌʌn

senses.definition: used to suggest that working together with others can often lead to better results than working alone, as combining different perspectives and ideas can create stronger solutions

examples.x: The business partners brought different skills and expertise to the table, recognizing that two heads are better than one when it comes to launching a successful venture.
